tripOverview.intro: London in spring offers the perfect romantic backdrop with blooming gardens and mild weather. This vibrant city combines historic charm with modern diversity, making it an ideal destination for couples.
tripOverview.vibe: A whirlwind romantic adventure through London's most iconic sights and hidden culinary gems.

destinationOverview.bestTime: Spring (April-June) offers mild weather and blooming gardens

attractions.name: Tower Bridge
attractions.desc: Iconic Victorian bridge with glass walkways and stunning Thames views.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.desc: World-class museum housing artifacts from ancient civilizations.

attractions.name: Borough Market
attractions.desc: Historic food market with artisanal produce and international cuisine.

attractions.name: Hyde Park
attractions.desc: Royal park perfect for romantic walks and Speaker's Corner.

attractions.name: Tate Modern
attractions.desc: Contemporary art gallery in a converted power station.

destinationOverview.transportation: London's extensive public transport includes the Underground (Tube), buses, and Overground trains covering all major attractions. Black cabs are iconic but expensive, while Uber and other ride-sharing apps are widely available and more affordable. An Oyster Card or contactless payment makes travel seamless across all transport modes.
destinationOverview.tip: Get an Oyster Card for convenient travel and always stand right on escalators - London is very LGBTQ+ friendly, especially in areas like Soho and Vauxhall.
destinationOverview.safety: London is very safe for tourists with low crime rates and excellent emergency services.
destinationOverview.airaloText: Stay connected with affordable eSIM data plans from Airalo for seamless navigation and sharing your London memories.

days.intro: Start your London adventure with iconic sights and incredible food at Borough Market.

morning.name: Tower of London
morning.desc: Explore the Crown Jewels and medieval fortress.

morning.name: Tower Bridge
morning.desc: Walk across the glass floor with panoramic views.

afternoon.name: Borough Market
afternoon.desc: Sample artisanal foods and vegetarian delights.

afternoon.name: Southwark Cathedral
afternoon.desc: Beautiful Gothic cathedral with Shakespeare connections.

evening.name: The George Inn
evening.desc: Historic pub with vegetarian options and craft ales.

evening.name: South Bank Walk
evening.desc: Romantic riverside stroll with city lights.

days.tip: Borough Market is busiest on weekends - arrive early for the best selection and shorter queues.

days.intro: Immerse yourself in London's royal heritage and world-class cultural institutions.

morning.name: British Museum
morning.desc: Marvel at ancient treasures and world artifacts.

morning.name: Russell Square
morning.desc: Peaceful garden square for a romantic break.

afternoon.name: Covent Garden
afternoon.desc: Street performers and charming market atmosphere.

afternoon.name: National Gallery
afternoon.desc: World-renowned art collection in Trafalgar Square.

evening.name: Soho Exploration
evening.desc: LGBTQ+ friendly neighborhood with diverse dining.

evening.name: The Admiral Duncan
evening.desc: Historic LGBTQ+ pub with welcoming atmosphere.

days.tip: Many major museums are free but consider donating - book timed slots online to skip queues.

days.intro: Experience London from the water and discover its vibrant arts scene.

morning.name: Thames River Cruise
morning.desc: Scenic boat ride with commentary and city views.

morning.name: Shakespeare's Globe
morning.desc: Historic theater replica with fascinating tours.

afternoon.name: Tate Modern
afternoon.desc: Contemporary art in stunning industrial setting.

afternoon.name: Millennium Bridge
afternoon.desc: Iconic pedestrian bridge with perfect photo ops.

evening.name: Gabriel's Wharf
evening.desc: Artisan shops and riverside dining options.

evening.name: The Anchor Bankside
evening.desc: Historic Thames-side pub with vegetarian menu.

days.tip: The Tate Modern's viewing level offers free panoramic city views - perfect for sunset photos.

days.intro: Final morning exploring London's vibrant market scene before departure.

morning.name: Camden Market
morning.desc: Eclectic market with vintage finds and street food.

morning.name: Regent's Canal Walk
morning.desc: Peaceful waterway stroll through trendy neighborhoods.

afternoon.name: King's Cross Station
afternoon.desc: Modern transport hub with shops and Platform 9¾.

afternoon.name: Airport Transfer
afternoon.desc: Heathrow Express or Tube journey to airport.

days.tip: Allow 2-3 hours for airport transfer and check-in - Heathrow Express is fastest but Piccadilly Line is cheaper.

food.intro: London's food scene brilliantly caters to vegetarians with innovative plant-based restaurants and diverse international cuisines.
food.tip: Many pubs now offer excellent vegetarian options, and Sunday roasts often include plant-based alternatives.

neighborhoods.name: South Bank
neighborhoods.desc: Riverside location near Borough Market and cultural attractions
neighborhoods.whyStay: Walking distance to markets and Thames views

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden
neighborhoods.desc: Central location with theaters, shops, and restaurants
neighborhoods.whyStay: Heart of London with excellent transport links

neighborhoods.name: King's Cross
neighborhoods.desc: Trendy area with modern amenities and easy airport access
neighborhoods.bestFor: Convenient departure and hip neighborhood vibes
neighborhoods.whyStay: Great restaurants and quick airport connections

budget.name: Premier Inn London County Hall
budget.vibe: Modern chain hotel with river views

budget.name: YHA London Central
budget.vibe: Clean hostel with private rooms available

midRange.name: Hub by Premier Inn Covent Garden
midRange.vibe: Compact modern rooms in prime location

luxury.name: Shangri-La at The Shard
luxury.vibe: Sky-high luxury with panoramic city views

luxury.name: Sea Containers London
luxury.vibe: Art deco glamour on the South Bank

hotels.tip: Book early for better rates, and consider hotels near Borough Market for easy access to vegetarian food options.

getYourGuide.name: Skip-the-Line Tower of London Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Fast-track entry with Beefeater guide and Crown Jewels access.

getYourGuide.name: Borough Market and London Bridge Food Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Guided tasting experience through London's most famous food market.

getYourGuide.name: Thames Sunset Cruise with Prosecco
getYourGuide.desc: Romantic evening boat ride with drinks and city skyline views.

hiddenGems.name: Neal's Yard
hiddenGems.desc: Colorful courtyard hidden in Covent Garden with organic shops and cafes.

hiddenGems.name: Leake Street Tunnel
hiddenGems.desc: Legal graffiti tunnel showcasing vibrant street art near Waterloo Station.

hiddenGems.name: Sky Garden
hiddenGems.desc: Free public garden with panoramic city views (advance booking required).

packing.title: April London Packing Guide
packing.intro: Pack layers for London's unpredictable spring weather with temperatures ranging from cool mornings to mild afternoons.
packing.luggage: A carry-on suitcase should suffice for a 4-day trip with efficient packing.
packing.tip: Pack a day bag for sightseeing and always carry an umbrella - London weather can change quickly.

bookTrip.intro: Round-trip flights from Philadelphia to London typically cost $600-800 per person in April.
